2606.18158.title: The Measurement Gap in the Automation of EU Law: Benchmarking Doctrinal Legal Reasoning under the EU AI Act
2606.18158.paper_summary: Large language models now produce legal text of at least median quality, yet no existing benchmark can evaluate whether they perform doctrinal legal reasoning, which forms the interpretive core of legal work, rather than the ancillary, paralegal tasks that most current legal-AI evaluations measure. This measurement gap is not only methodological but legal: the EU AI Act makes "appropriate accuracy" a binding requirement for high-risk AI used in the judicial domain, yet that requirement cannot acquire operational content without the very doctrinal-reasoning benchmark the field lacks.
2606.18158.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型現在能夠產生至少中位數品質的法律文本，然而現有的基準無法評估它們是否進行了法律學說推理，這是法律工作的詮釋核心，而不是大多數當前法律人工智慧評估所測量的輔助性、法律助理任務。這一測量差距不僅是方法論上的，也是法律上的：歐盟人工智慧法案將「適當的準確性」作為司法領域中高風險人工智慧的強制性要求，然而該要求在沒有該領域所缺乏的法律學說推理基準的情況下，無法獲得操作性內容。
2606.18158.author: Michèle Finck et.al.
